steps.title: Use the vertex x-coordinate to narrow the field
steps.content:
All four choices are upward-opening parabolas with leading coefficient 2. The vertex $x$-coordinate is $x=-\dfrac{b}{2a}$.
  • Choices A and B have $b=-11$, giving vertex $x=\dfrac{11}{4}=2.75$.
  • Choices C and D have $b=-5$, giving vertex $x=\dfrac{5}{4}=1.25$.
The scatterplot's minimum sits near $x\approx 3$, much closer to 2.75 than to 1.25, so choices C and D do not fit.

steps.title: Test a visible scatterplot point to choose between A and B
steps.content:
A clear point on the scatterplot is near $(5,15)$.
  • A) $y=2(5)^{2}-11(5)-20=50-55-20=-25$, far below 15 — eliminate.
  • B) $y=2(5)^{2}-11(5)+20=50-55+20=15$, matches the point exactly.
  • C) Vertex at $x=1.25$ misses the plotted minimum near $x=3$ — eliminate.
  • D) Vertex at $x=1.25$ misses the plotted minimum near $x=3$ — eliminate.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
